params.reason: Chapter 71 Note: Headings 71.13, 71.14, 71.15 exclude articles with precious metal/metal clad with precious metal as minor constituents only (minor fittings or ornamentation); Heading 71.16 excludes articles containing precious metal/metal clad with precious metal other than as minor constituents; Headings 71.13, 71.14, 71.15 exclude articles of base metal or precious metal merely plated with precious metal

Chapter 71 Note: articles with precious metal or metal clad with precious metal as minor constituents only (minor fittings or ornamentation) are excluded from headings 71.13, 71.14, 71.15; articles containing precious metal or metal clad with precious metal are excluded from heading 71.16
Amalgams of precious metal, or colloidal precious metal are excluded and classified in heading 28.43
sterile surgical suture materials, dental fillings and other goods of Chapter 30 are excluded
Goods of Chapter 32 (for example, lustres) are excluded from this section/chapter
Reference to articles of headings 42.02 or 42.03 as specified in Note 3 (B) to Chapter 42 are excluded from the current scope
Articles of headings 43.03 and 43.04 are excluded from the current section or chapter
Goods of Section XI (textiles and textile articles) are excluded from the current section
footwear, headgear or other articles of Chapter 64 or 65 are excluded
Umbrellas, walking-sticks and other articles of Chapter 66 are excluded from the current section/chapter

Chapter 71 Note: abrasive goods of headings 68.04, 68.05 or Chapter 82 containing precious/semi-precious stone dust/powder; articles of Chapter 82 with working parts of precious/semi-precious stones; machinery, mechanical appliances or electrical goods of Section XVI are excluded, except wholly precious/semi-precious stone articles and parts (which remain in Chapter 71), except unmounted worked sapphires and diamonds for styli (heading 85.22)
Articles of Chapters 90, 91, or 92 (scientific instruments, clocks and watches, musical instruments) are excluded from the current Section/Chapter
Chapter 93: arms or parts thereof are excluded from this section/chapter
Reference to Note 2 of Chapter 95: articles covered by that note are excluded from the current section/chapter
Articles classified in Chapter 96 by virtue of Note 4 to Chapter 96 are excluded

Section XXI Note: original sculptures or statuary, collectors' pieces, and antiques of age exceeding one hundred years are excluded, except natural or cultured pearls or precious or semi-precious stones

params.note: Subject to Note 1(a) to Section VI

conditions.value: classify with predominant metal by weight

params.covers: articles consisting wholly or partly of precious metal or of metal clad with precious metal; articles of precious metal or of metal clad with precious metal set with natural or cultured pearls, precious or semi-precious stones

conditions.value: natural or cultured pearls, precious or semi-precious stones

params.covers: articles of natural or cultured pearls or of precious or semi-precious stones (natural, synthetic or reconstructed)
articles of precious metal or of metal clad with precious metal

params.term: precious metal, platinum, precious or semi-precious stones
params.meaning: (A) precious metal means silver, gold and platinum; (B) platinum means platinum, iridium, osmium, palladium, rhodium and ruthenium; (C) precious or semi-precious stones excludes substances specified in Chapter 96 Note 2(b)
an alloy containing 2% or more, by weight, of platinum
an alloy containing 2% or more by weight of gold but no platinum, or less than 2% by weight of platinum
alloys containing 2% or more, by weight, of silver
includes alloys treated as alloys of precious metal in accordance with Note 5; excludes articles defined in Note 7, and base metal or glass plated with precious metal
exclusions: base metal plated with precious metal

params.meaning: material made with a base of metal upon one or more surfaces of which there is affixed by soldering, brazing, welding, hot-rolling or similar mechanical means a covering of precious metal; also covers base metal inlaid with precious metal
params.scope: general definition applying throughout the Nomenclature

params.meaning: rings, bracelets, necklaces, brooches, ear-rings, watch-chains, fobs, pendants, tie-pins, cuff-links, dress-studs, religious or other medals and insignia

params.term: articles of personal use of a kind normally carried in the pocket, in the handbag or on the person
params.meaning: articles designed to be carried in pocket, handbag or on person, including cigar/cigarette cases, snuff boxes, cachou/pill boxes, powder compacts, chain purses, prayer beads

params.term: articles of goldsmiths' or silversmiths' wares
params.meaning: includes ornaments, tableware, toilet-ware, smokers' requisites and other articles of household, office or religious use

articles of jewellery within the meaning of paragraph (a) of Note 9, excluding buttons, dress-combs, hair-slides, hairpins, and not incorporating natural or cultured pearls, precious or semi-precious stones, or precious metal (except as plating or minor constituents)
products of which 90 % or more by weight passes through a sieve having a mesh aperture of 0.5 mm
does not include iridium, osmium, palladium, rhodium or ruthenium
